description: Low-growing plants with narrow leaves that cover the ground in meadows, lawns, and prairies. 'Whelina' names the humble abundance—the green carpet that softens earth, feeds grazers, holds soil, and waves in every breeze.
sound_symbolism: The word waves and spreads. 'Whe' /ʍe/ begins with breathy softness, grass stirring in wind. Then 'li' lightens with liquid life, the countless blades. Finally 'na' touches ground, the grass rooted and spreading.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo whelina' (the grass). Takes the vegetal classifier 'lipha' when counted as types or areas. Often uncountable when referring to ground cover generally. Central to prairie, meadow, and pastoral vocabulary.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Grasslands sustained the great herds and the peoples who followed them—grass as foundation of entire ways of life.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Grass teaches humility and resilience—it bends but does not break, thrives through being walked upon, succeeds through abundance rather than height.
